revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年4月}} '''賢所'''（かしこどころ/けんしょ）とは、日本の天皇が居住する宮中において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである[[八咫鏡]]を祀る場所。
== 概要 == [[八咫鏡]]そのものを祀るというより、[[八咫鏡]]を[[天照大神|天照大御神]]の神魂として祀るといった方が正しい。かつては[[内侍]]が管理したため'''内侍所'''（ないしどころ）とも称され、威所・尊所・恐所・畏所などともいった。現在は[[宮中三殿]]の中央が賢所とされる。なお、「かしこどころ」で神鏡そのものを、「けんしょ」で宮中三殿そのものを指すことがある。
== 歴史 == 起源としては、[[伊勢神宮]]に奉安されている八咫鏡の模造の神鏡が天皇の居住する[[内裏]]の側に奉安され、賢所とされるようになった。
[[平安京]]の内裏では[[温明殿]]の南側の身舎が賢所とされた。
[[平安時代]]中期以後、温明殿と[[綾綺殿]]との間の西側の庭において毎年[[12月_(旧暦)|12月]]に[[天皇]]臨席のもと[[御神楽]]が開かれた。
後に内裏が荒廃すると、賢所は[[春興殿]]に移された。
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:皇室の施設]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[平安京]]の内裏では[[温明殿]]の南側の身舎が賢所とされた。

== 歴史 == 起源としては、[[崇神天皇]]の御代に八咫鏡を皇居外に移し、[[垂仁天皇]]の御代に[[倭姫命]]により現在の[[伊勢神宮]]へ、模造の神鏡（方代）が皇居へ祀られた。後に[[内裏]]の側に奉安され、賢所とされるようになった。
[[平安京]]の内裏では[[温明殿]]の南側の身舎が賢所とされた。
[[平安時代]]中期以後、温明殿と[[綾綺殿]]との間の西側の庭において毎年[[12月_(旧暦)|12月]]に[[天皇]]臨席のもと[[御神楽]]が開かれた。
後に内裏が荒廃すると、賢所は[[春興殿]]に移された。

{{出典の明記|date=2011年4月}} '''賢所'''（かしこどころ／けんしょ）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである[[八咫鏡]]を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。

それ以後、温明殿と[[綾綺殿]]との間の西側の庭において毎年[[12月_(旧暦)|12月]]に[[天皇]]臨席のもと[[御神楽]]が開かれた。

{{出典の明記|date=2011年4月}} '''賢所'''（かしこどころ／けんしょ）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである'''[[八咫鏡]]'''を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 八咫鏡そのものを祀るというより、八咫鏡を'''[[天照大神|天照大御神]]'''の[[御霊代]]として祀るといった方が正しい。かつては[[内侍]]が管理したため'''内侍所'''（ないしどころ）とも称され、威所・尊所・恐所・畏所などともいった。現在は[[宮中三殿]]の中央が賢所とされる。なお、「かしこどころ」で神鏡そのものを、「けんしょ」で宮中三殿そのものを指すことがある。3つの部屋に分かれており、手前から外陣、内陣、内内陣と呼ばれる。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋、内内陣は天照大神を祀っている部屋である。
== 歴史 == 起源としては、[[崇神天皇]]の御代に八咫鏡を皇居外の笠縫邑に移したが、皇居では神鏡の形代（かたしろ）が作られ祭祀が続けられた。 *なお笠縫邑の八咫鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の御代に[[倭姫命]]により伊勢へ移動して祀られた。これが現在の'''[[伊勢神宮]]'''の起源である。 宮中の形代は、[[平安時代]]前期まで天皇の生活の場である[[内裏]]の[[仁寿殿]]内で祀られていたが、[[宇多天皇]]の御代に天皇の生活の場は[[清涼殿]]に移ると同時に、神鏡は天皇の身辺から分離され[[温明殿]]内の南側の身舎に奉安され、ここが賢所と称されるようになった。
それ以後、温明殿と[[綾綺殿]]との間の西側の庭において毎年[[12月_(旧暦)|12月]]に[[天皇]]臨席のもと[[御神楽]]が開かれた。 後に内裏が荒廃すると、賢所は[[春興殿]]に移された。
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]
{{神道 横}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:平安時代]] [[Category:皇室の施設]] [[Category:三種の神器]]

revision.comment: 辞典空間に貴賤の別は無く、いかなる相手にも中立のため、本文中は[[Wikipedia:スタイルマニュアル (人物伝)|丁寧語表現は付けない]]。
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年4月}} '''賢所'''（かしこどころ／けんしょ）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである'''[[八咫鏡]]'''を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 八咫鏡そのものを祀るというより、八咫鏡を'''[[天照大神|天照大御神]]'''の[[御霊代]]として祀るといった方が正しい。かつては[[内侍]]が管理したため'''内侍所'''（ないしどころ）とも称され、威所・尊所・恐所・畏所などともいった。現在は[[宮中三殿]]の中央が賢所とされる。なお、「かしこどころ」で神鏡そのものを、「けんしょ」で宮中三殿そのものを指すことがある。3つの部屋に分かれており、手前から外陣、内陣、内内陣と呼ばれる。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋、内内陣は天照大神を祀っている部屋である。
== 歴史 == 起源としては、[[崇神天皇]]の代に八咫鏡を皇居外の笠縫邑に移したが、皇居では神鏡の形代（かたしろ）が作られ祭祀が続けられた。
なお笠縫邑の八咫鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[倭姫命]]により伊勢へ移動して祀られた。これが現在の'''[[伊勢神宮]]'''の起源である。
宮中の形代は、[[平安時代]]前期まで天皇の生活の場である[[内裏]]の[[仁寿殿]]内で祀られていたが、[[宇多天皇]]の代に天皇の生活の場は[[清涼殿]]に移ると同時に、神鏡は天皇の身辺から分離され[[温明殿]]内の南側の身舎に奉安され、ここが賢所と称されるようになった。
それ以後、温明殿と[[綾綺殿]]との間の西側の庭において毎年[[12月_(旧暦)|12月]]に[[天皇]]臨席のもと[[御神楽]]が開かれた。
後に内裏が荒廃すると、賢所は[[春興殿]]に移された。
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]

{{出典の明記|date=2011年4月}} '''賢所'''（かしこどころ、けんしょ）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである'''[[八咫鏡]]'''を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 八咫鏡そのものを祀るというより、八咫鏡を'''[[天照大神|天照大御神]]'''の[[御霊代]]として祀るといった方が正しい。かつては[[内侍]]が管理したため'''内侍所'''（ないしどころ）とも称され、威所・尊所・恐所・畏所などともいった。現在は[[宮中三殿]]の中央が賢所とされる。なお、「かしこどころ」で神鏡そのものを、「けんしょ」で宮中三殿そのものを指すことがある。3つの部屋に分かれており、手前から外陣、内陣、内内陣と呼ばれる。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋、内内陣は天照大神を祀っている部屋である。
== 歴史 == 起源としては、[[崇神天皇]]の代に八咫鏡を皇居外の笠縫邑に移したが、皇居では神鏡の形代（かたしろ）が作られ祭祀が続けられた。
なお笠縫邑の八咫鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[倭姫命]]により伊勢へ移動して祀られた。これが現在の[[伊勢神宮]]の起源である。
宮中の形代は、[[平安時代]]前期まで天皇の生活の場である[[内裏]]の[[仁寿殿]]内で祀られていたが、[[宇多天皇]]の代に天皇の生活の場は[[清涼殿]]に移ると同時に、神鏡は天皇の身辺から分離され[[温明殿]]内の南側の身舎に奉安され、ここが賢所と称されるようになった。
それ以後、温明殿と[[綾綺殿]]との間の西側の庭において毎年[[12月_(旧暦)|12月]]に[[天皇]]臨席のもと[[御神楽]]が開かれた。
後に内裏が荒廃すると、賢所は[[春興殿]]に移された。
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]
{{神道 横}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:平安時代]] [[Category:皇室の施設]] [[Category:三種の神器]]

'''賢所'''（かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=2}}）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである神鏡（[[八咫鏡]]）を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 宮中三殿は南面して建てられており、中央に賢所、西に皇霊殿、東に神殿が位置しており、それぞれ外陣、内陣、内々陣（内内陣）に分かれている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=43}}。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋で、賢所の内々陣（内内陣）は天照大神を祀る部屋である。
歴史的には内侍所（ないしどころ）とも称され、「かしこどころ」には「恐所」・「畏所」・「威所」・「尊所」・「貴所」など種々の文字が当てられた{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=1-2}}。
賢所の内陣には2基の常夜灯があり絶やされることなく明かりが灯されている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=42}}。
== 歴史 == 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば第十代[[崇神天皇]]の代に「共住不安」として神鏡を皇居外に移して祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto">[https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf 橋本富太郎「宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について」モラロジー研究 No.55（2005）] 、2022年7月27日閲覧。</ref>（この神鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[伊勢国]][[五十鈴川]]上流に移されのちに[[伊勢神宮]]となる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=4}}）。その際に『[[古語拾遺]]』によると宮中で祀るための神鏡の分身を改めて鋳造し宮中に祀られることになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。
神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|year=1909}} * {{Citation|和書|author=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号 2017年夏|publisher=扶桑社|year=2017}}
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:平安時代]] [[Category:皇室の施設]] [[Category:三種の神器]]

'''賢所'''（かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=2}}）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである神鏡（[[八咫鏡]]）を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 宮中三殿は南面して建てられており、中央に賢所、西に皇霊殿、東に神殿が位置しており、それぞれ外陣、内陣、内々陣（内内陣）に分かれている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=43}}。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋で、賢所の内々陣（内内陣）は天照大神を祀る部屋である。
歴史的には内侍所（ないしどころ）とも称され、「かしこどころ」には「恐所」・「畏所」・「威所」・「尊所」・「貴所」など種々の文字が当てられた{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=1-2}}。
賢所の内陣には2基の常夜灯があり絶やされることなく明かりが灯されている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=42}}。
== 歴史 == 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば第十代[[崇神天皇]]の代に「共住不安」として神鏡を皇居外に移して祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto">[https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf 橋本富太郎「宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について」モラロジー研究 No.55（2005）] 、2022年7月27日閲覧。</ref>（この神鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[伊勢国]][[五十鈴川]]上流に移されのちに[[伊勢神宮]]となる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=4}}）。その際に『[[古語拾遺]]』によると宮中で祀るための神鏡の分身を改めて鋳造し宮中に祀られることになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。
神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。別殿に移された時期について、『[[江家次第]]』や『[[禁秘抄]]』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『[[撰集抄]]』は[[宇多天皇]]の代としている<ref name="hashimoto" />。
平安時代中期以降、内裏の焼失等の理由で[[里内裏]]が定められたが、それに伴って賢所も移動し、適宜の殿舎が充てられた<ref name="hashimoto" />。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|year=1909}} * {{Citation|和書|author=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号 2017年夏|publisher=扶桑社|year=2017}}
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:平安時代]] [[Category:皇室の施設]] [[Category:三種の神器]]

'''賢所'''（かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=2}}）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]のひとつである神鏡（[[八咫鏡]]）を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 宮中三殿は南面して建てられており、中央に賢所、西に皇霊殿、東に神殿が位置しており、それぞれ外陣、内陣、内々陣（内内陣）に分かれている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=43}}。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋で、賢所の内々陣（内内陣）は天照大神を祀る部屋である。
歴史的には内侍所（ないしどころ）とも称され、「かしこどころ」には「恐所」・「畏所」・「威所」・「尊所」・「貴所」など種々の文字が当てられた{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=1-2}}。
賢所の内陣には2基の常夜灯があり絶やされることなく明かりが灯されている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=42}}。
== 歴史 == 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば第十代[[崇神天皇]]の代に「共住不安」として神鏡を皇居外に移して祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto">[https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf 橋本富太郎「宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について」モラロジー研究 No.55（2005）] 、2022年7月27日閲覧。</ref>（この神鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[伊勢国]][[五十鈴川]]上流に移されのちに[[伊勢神宮]]となる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=4}}）。その際に『[[古語拾遺]]』によると宮中で祀るための神鏡の分身を改めて鋳造し宮中に祀られることになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。
神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。別殿に移された時期について、『[[江家次第]]』や『[[禁秘抄]]』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『[[撰集抄]]』は[[宇多天皇]]の代としている<ref name="hashimoto" />。
平安時代中期以降、[[内裏]]の焼失等の理由で[[里内裏]]が定められたが、それに伴って賢所も移動し、適宜の殿舎が充てられた<ref name="hashimoto" />。
さらに後世には[[春興殿]]に賢所が移され、その記述がある洞院実熙の『[[名目抄]]』から室町時代の初期には春興殿が賢所となっていた{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=12}}。
明治初期に東京に遷り、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）9月の詔により賢所を含む神殿が造営されることになった{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=13-15}}。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]

神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。別殿に移された時期について、『[[江家次第]]』や『[[禁秘抄]]』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『[[撰集抄]]』は[[宇多天皇]]の代としている<ref name="hashimoto" />。
平安時代中期以降、[[内裏]]の焼失等の理由で[[里内裏]]が定められたが、それに伴って賢所も移動し、適宜の殿舎が充てられた<ref name="hashimoto" />。
さらに後世には[[春興殿]]に賢所が移され、その記述がある洞院実熙の『[[名目抄]]』から室町時代の初期には春興殿が賢所となっていたとみられる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=12}}。
明治初期に東京に遷り、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）9月の詔により賢所を含む神殿が造営されることになった{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=13-15}}。

{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:平安時代]] [[Category:皇室の施設]] [[Category:三種の神器]]

'''賢所'''（かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=2}}）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]の一つであり、[[天照大神]]の御霊代（[[神体]]）とする神鏡（[[八咫鏡]]）を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 宮中三殿は南面して建てられており、中央に賢所、西に皇霊殿、東に神殿が位置しており、それぞれ外陣、内陣、内々陣（内内陣）に分かれている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=43}}。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋で、賢所の内々陣（内内陣）は天照大神を祀る部屋である。
歴史的には内侍所（ないしどころ）とも称され、「かしこどころ」には「恐所」・「畏所」・「威所」・「尊所」・「貴所」など種々の文字が当てられた{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=1-2}}。
賢所の内陣には2基の常夜灯があり絶やされることなく明かりが灯されている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=42}}。
== 歴史 == 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば第十代[[崇神天皇]]の代に「共住不安」として神鏡を皇居外に移して祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto">[https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf 橋本富太郎「宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について」モラロジー研究 No.55（2005）] 、2022年7月27日閲覧。</ref>（この神鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[伊勢国]][[五十鈴川]]上流に移されのちに[[伊勢神宮]]となる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=4}}）。その際に『[[古語拾遺]]』によると宮中で祀るための神鏡の分身を改めて鋳造し宮中に祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。
神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。別殿に移された時期について、『[[江家次第]]』や『[[禁秘抄]]』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『[[撰集抄]]』は[[宇多天皇]]の代としている<ref name="hashimoto" />。
平安時代中期以降、[[内裏]]の焼失等の理由で[[里内裏]]が定められたが、それに伴って賢所も移動し、適宜の殿舎が充てられた<ref name="hashimoto" />。
さらに後世には[[春興殿]]に賢所が移され、その記述がある洞院実熙の『[[名目抄]]』から室町時代の初期には春興殿が賢所となっていたとみられる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=12}}。
明治初期に東京に遷り、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）9月の詔により賢所を含む神殿が造営されることになった{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=13-15}}。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|year=1909}} * {{Citation|和書|author=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号 2017年夏|publisher=扶桑社|year=2017}}
== 関連項目 == * [[賢所乗御車|賢所奉安車]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしこところ}} [[Category:平安時代]] [[Category:皇室の施設]] [[Category:三種の神器]]

revision.text:
'''賢所'''（かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=2}}）とは、[[日本]]の[[天皇]]が居住する[[宮中]]において、[[三種の神器]]の一つであり、[[天照大神]]の御霊代（[[神体]]）とする神鏡（[[八咫鏡]]）を祀る場所。現在の[[皇居]]においては[[宮中三殿]]の一つである。
== 概要 == 宮中三殿は南面して建てられており、中央に賢所、西に皇霊殿、東に神殿が位置しており、それぞれ外陣、内陣、内々陣（内内陣）に分かれている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=43}}。外陣は皇族のみが入ることが許される部屋、内陣は天皇・皇后のみが入ることが許される部屋で、賢所の内々陣（内内陣）は天照大神を祀る部屋である。
歴史的には内侍所（ないしどころ）とも称され、「かしこどころ」には「恐所」・「畏所」・「威所」・「尊所」・「貴所」など種々の文字が当てられた{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=1-2}}。
賢所の内陣には2基の常夜灯があり絶やされることなく明かりが灯されている{{Sfn|皇室75号|2017|p=42}}。
== 歴史 == [[天照大神]]から授けられた宝鏡は「同床共殿」神勅により皇孫以来代々皇居内に奉安されてきた{{Sfn|皇室と神宮|S32|p=4}}。
『[[日本書紀]]』によれば第十代[[崇神天皇]]の代に「共住不安」として神鏡を皇居外に移して祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto">[https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf 橋本富太郎「宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について」モラロジー研究 No.55（2005）] 、2022年7月27日閲覧。</ref>（この神鏡は[[垂仁天皇]]の代に[[伊勢国]][[五十鈴川]]上流に移されのちに[[伊勢神宮]]となる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=4}}）。その際に『[[古語拾遺]]』によると宮中で祀るための神鏡の分身を改めて鋳造し宮中に祀ることになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。
神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった<ref name="hashimoto" />。別殿に移された時期について、『[[江家次第]]』や『[[禁秘抄]]』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『[[撰集抄]]』は[[宇多天皇]]の代としている<ref name="hashimoto" />。
平安時代中期以降、[[内裏]]の焼失等の理由で[[里内裏]]が定められたが、それに伴って賢所も移動し、適宜の殿舎が充てられた<ref name="hashimoto" />。
さらに後世には[[春興殿]]に賢所が移され、その記述がある洞院実熙の『[[名目抄]]』から室町時代の初期には春興殿が賢所となっていたとみられる{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=12}}。
明治初期に東京に遷り、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）9月の詔により賢所を含む神殿が造営されることになった{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1909|p=13-15}}。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|year=1909}} * {{Citation|和書|author=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号 2017年夏|publisher=扶桑社|year=2017}} * {{Citation|和書|author=木村大樹|date=2022-1-1|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=吉川弘文館|ref={{SfnRef|古代天皇祭祀の研究|2022}} }} * {{Citation|和書|author=八束清貫|date=S32-6-1|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|ref={{SfnRef|古皇室と神宮|S32}} }}

revision.text:
{{draft}}
'''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''Yata no Kagami''', one of the '''Three Sacred Treasures''' (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''Amaterasu''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[Tentensha|展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[Kodansha|講談社]]|series=[[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} ; Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ; Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017年夏|publisher=[[Fusosha|扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} * {{Shinto|ja|神道}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashikodokoro}} [[ja:%E8%B3%A2%E6%89%80]] [[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{notelist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashiko dokoro-qq]] [[Category:Kashiko dokoro]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashiko dokoro-qq]] [[Category:Kashiko dokoro]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashiko dokoro-qq]] [[Category:Kashiko dokoro]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kashiko dokoro]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:Kashiko dokoro]][[en:Kashiko dokoro]][[en:Kashiko dokoro]] [[en:Kashiko dokoro]]}} == Overview == The '''Kyūchū Sanden''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == Related Topics == * {{draft}}
'''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == Overview == The '''Kyūchū Sanden''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro '''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿}} * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿}} * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{draft}}
'''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. dokoro]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashiko dokoro]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:賢所]]

{{draft}}
'''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿}} * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{draft}}
'''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿}} * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashikodokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashikodokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashikodokoro Misogi|Kashikodokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashikodokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashikodokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashikodokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashikodokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro {{draft}}
'''Kashikodokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashikodokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashikodokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashikodokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashikodokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashikodokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashikodokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashikodokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashikodokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashikodokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashikodokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashikodokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashikodokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. The '''Kashikodokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashikodokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashikodokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashikodokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashikodokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿}} * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations ===
