revision.text:
{{draft}}
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == {{draft}}
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == {{draft}}
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing imported pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}}
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * revision.text:
{{draft}}
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[Tentensha|展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[Kodansha|講談社]]|series=[[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} ; Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ; Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[Fusosha|扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} * {{Shinto|ja|神道}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{notelist}} == See Also == * == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|3}} == Bibliography == ;Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[講談社]]|series=[[講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|author=中本真人|date=2021-12-22|title=なぜ神楽は応仁の乱を乗り越えられたのか|publisher=新典社|ref={{SfnRef|中本真人|2021}} }} ;Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ;Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|ja|賢所乗御車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|賢所|version=101852757|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[sn:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[ja:%E8%B3%A2%E6%89%80]] [[ja:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[zh:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[fr:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[uk:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[sn:Kashiko dokoro]] [[en:Kashiko dokoro]] [[ja:賢所]] [[sn:ashiko dokoro]] [[en:Kashiko-dokoro]]

revision.text: '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata no Kagami|qq=}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata no Kagami|qq=}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu Omikami]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarashina Nikki|qq=}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Tale of Genji|qq=}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu Omikami]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal lamps|qq=}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|qq=}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/更級日記|qq=}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/源氏物語|qq=}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|qq=}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|qq=}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|qq=}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|qq=}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/宇多天皇|qq=}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|qq=}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu Omikami]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/垂仁天皇|qq=}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/伊勢国|qq=}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/五十鈴川|qq=}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/伊勢神宮|qq=}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shuui|qq=}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|qq=}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|qq=}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|qq=}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kashiko dokoro]] [[sn:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[ja:%E8%B3%A2%E6%89%80]] [[ja:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[zh:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[fr:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[uk:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[sn:Kashiko dokoro]] [[en:Kashiko dokoro]] [[ja:賢所]] [[sn:ashiko dokoro]] [[en:Kashiko-dokoro]]

'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata no Kagami|qq=}}''', one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Emperor]]'s [[imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|ja|八咫鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata no Kagami|qq=}}), one of the [[Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|ja|更級日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarashina Nikki|qq=}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|ja|源氏物語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Tale of Genji|qq=}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|eternal lamps|ja|常夜灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal lamps|qq=}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|qq=}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|更級日記|ja|更級日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/更級日記|qq=}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|源氏物語|ja|源氏物語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/源氏物語|qq=}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|qq=}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|qq=}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|qq=}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|qq=}}』は{{ill|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/宇多天皇|qq=}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|qq=}} and [[Shinto ritual|shinji]] are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|垂仁天皇|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/垂仁天皇|qq=}}の代に{{ill|伊勢国|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/伊勢国|qq=}}の{{ill|五十鈴川|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/五十鈴川|qq=}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|伊勢神宮|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/伊勢神宮|qq=}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shuui|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shuui|qq=}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|qq=}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|qq=}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|qq=}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * Japan|ja|天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kyūchū Sanden|ja|宮中三殿|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūchū
