[[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]]
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami}}''', one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor}}'s [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami}}), one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=江家次第|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|WD=Q109937652|lt=shinji}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=伊勢国}}の{{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shuui}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|1=Shunkōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513798}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|1=Sanehiro Toin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555877}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} [[ja:賢所]] == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]]
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami}}''', one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor}}'s [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami}}), one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=更級日記}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=源氏物語}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=江家次第|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|WD=Q109937652|lt=shinji}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=垂仁天皇}}の代に{{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=伊勢国}}の{{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=五十鈴川}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=伊勢神宮}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shuui}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|1=Shunkōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513798}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|1=Sanehiro Toin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555877}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} [[ja:賢所]] == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[Tentensha|展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[Kodansha|講談社]]|series=[[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} ; Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ; Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[Fusosha|扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} * {{Shinto|ja|神道}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{notelist}} == See Also == * == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|3}} == Bibliography == ;Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[講談社]]|series=[[講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|author=中本真人|date=2021-12-22|title=なぜ神楽は応仁の乱を乗り越えられたのか|publisher=新典社|ref={{SfnRef|中本真人|2021}} }} ;Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ;Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|賢所|version=101852757|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| }} [[ja:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[zh:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[fr:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[uk:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[ja:賢所]] {{wikidata link|Q11635085}} [[Category:Heian period]] [[Category:Japanese Imperial Facilities]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]]
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}''', one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor}}'s [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}), one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki|1=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji|1=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps|1=Nightlight}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=更級日記|1=Sarashina Nikki}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=源氏物語|1=The Tale of Genji}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇|1=Emperor Uda}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|WD=Q109937652|lt=shinji|1=Shinto ritual}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=垂仁天皇|1=Emperor Suinin}}の代に{{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=伊勢国|1=Ise Province}}の{{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=五十鈴川|1=Isuzu River}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shuui|1=Kogo Shūi}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|1=Shunkōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513798}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|1=Sanehiro Tōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555877}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} [[ja:賢所]] == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]]
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}''', one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor}}'s [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}), one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki|1=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji|1=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps|1=Nightlight}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=更級日記|1=Sarashina Nikki}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=源氏物語|1=The Tale of Genji}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇|1=Emperor Uda}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|WD=Q109937652|lt=shinji|1=Shinto ritual}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} [[ja:賢所]] == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[Tentensha|展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[Kodansha|講談社]]|series=[[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} ; Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[Fusosha|扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} * {{Shinto|ja|神道}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{notelist}} == See Also == * == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|3}} == Bibliography == ;Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[講談社]]|series=[[講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|author=中本真人|date=2021-12-22|title=なぜ神楽は応仁の乱を乗り越えられたのか|publisher=新典社|ref={{SfnRef|中本真人|2021}} }} ;Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ;Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|賢所|version=101852757|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| }} [[ja:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[zh:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[fr:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[uk:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[ja:賢所]] {{wikidata link|Q11635085}} [[Category:Heian period]] [[Category:Japanese Imperial Facilities]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]]
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}''', one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor}}'s [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}), one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki|1=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji|1=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps|1=Nightlight}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|WD=Q217955|lt=更級日記|1=Sarashina Nikki}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=源氏物語|1=The Tale of Genji}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇|1=Emperor Uda}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|WD=Q109937652|lt=shinji|1=Shinto ritual}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=垂仁天皇|1=Emperor Suinin}}の代に{{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=伊勢国|1=Ise Province}}の{{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=五十鈴川|1=Isuzu River}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shuui|1=Kogo Shūi}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|1=Shunkōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513798}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|1=Sanehiro Tōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555877}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} [[ja:賢所]] == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[Tentensha|展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[Kodansha|講談社]]|series=[[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} ; Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ; Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[Fusosha|扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} * {{Shinto|ja|神道}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{notelist}} == See Also == * == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|3}} == Bibliography == ;Books * {{Cite book|和書|author=皇典講究所|title=宮中三殿並に祝祭日解説|publisher=国晃館|date=1908-11|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=八束清貫|title=皇室と神宮|publisher=神宮司廳教導部|series=神宮教養叢書4|date=1957-06|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=松山能夫|title=明治維新神道百年史|publisher=神道文化会|date=1966-4|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=中澤伸弘|title=宮中祭祀：連綿と続く天皇の祈り|publisher=[[展転社]]|date=2010-07|isbn=9784886563460|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=藤田覚|title=江戸時代の天皇|publisher=[[講談社]]|series=[[講談社学術文庫]]|date=2018-05|origdate=2011-06|isbn=9784065116401|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=木村大樹|title=古代天皇祭祀の研究|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|date=2022-01|isbn=9784642046657|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|author=中本真人|date=2021-12-22|title=なぜ神楽は応仁の乱を乗り越えられたのか|publisher=新典社|ref={{SfnRef|中本真人|2021}} }} ;Papers * {{Cite journal|和書|author=橋本富太郎|title=宮中賢所における祭祀の草創期について|journal=モラロジー研究：倫理道徳研究フォーラム|publisher=道徳科学研究所|issue=55|pages=51-78|date=2005-02|url=https://www.moralogy.jp/wp-content/themes/mor/img_research/55hashimoto.pdf|ref=harv}} ;Magazines * {{Cite book|和書|editor=皇室編集部|title=皇室75号：2017th year 夏|publisher=[[扶桑社]]|date=2017-07|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|WD=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|賢所|version=101852757|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| }} [[ja:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[zh:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[fr:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[uk:Kashiko-dokoro]] [[ja:賢所]] {{wikidata link|Q11635085}} [[Category:Heian period]] [[Category:Japanese Imperial Facilities]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]

[[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]] [[ja:賢所]]
'''Kashiko-dokoro''' (賢所, also read as Kensho{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is the location within the '''Imperial Palace''' (宮中) of Japan where the '''{{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|qid=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}''', one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|'''Three Sacred Treasures''']] (三種の神器) and the sacred mirror of the goddess '''[[Amaterasu]]''', is enshrined. Currently, it is one of the '''Kyūchū Sanden''' (宮中三殿) in the '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Before the modern era, it was also called '''Naishidokoro''' (内侍所){{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. '''Kashiko-dokoro''' (かしこどころ、けんしょ{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=2}}) is a place in the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|qid=Q39018|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor}}'s [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] where the sacred mirror ({{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|qid=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}), one of the [[:en:Three Sacred Treasures of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures of Japan]] and the spirit of [[Amaterasu]], is enshrined. In the current [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] it is one of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|qid=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. [[File:Three Palace Sanctuaries.jpg|290px]] == Overview == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == Overview == The {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|qid=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} '''(Kyūchū Sanden)''' face south and consist of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' at the center, the '''Kōreiden''' to the west, and the '''Shinden''' to the east{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Among these three sanctuaries, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' is regarded as the most sacred, and its floor is raised approximately one shaku (about 30 centimeters) higher than the others{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|qid=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki|1=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|qid=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji|1=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|qid=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps|1=Nightlight}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|qid=Q217955|lt=更級日記|1=Sarashina Nikki}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|qid=Q8269|lt=源氏物語|1=The Tale of Genji}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇|1=Emperor Uda}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|qid=Q109937652|lt=shinji|1=Shinto ritual}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=垂仁天皇|1=Emperor Suinin}}の代に{{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=伊勢国|1=Ise Province}}の{{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|qid=Q6089522|lt=五十鈴川|1=Isuzu River}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shuui|1=Kogo Shūi}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3012148}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|1=Shunkōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513798}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|1=Sanehiro Tōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555877}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|qid=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|qid=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} [[ja:賢所]] == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} ===References=== {{Reflist|3}} == References == ; The structure is divided into three sections: * '''Outer Chamber''' (外陣): Accessible only to members of the Imperial family. * '''Inner Chamber''' (内陣): Accessible only to the Emperor and Empress. * '''Innermost Chamber''' (内々陣): The space where the sacred mirror and '''Amaterasu''' are enshrined. The '''Inner Chamber''' of the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' features two '''eternal lamps''' (常夜灯){{Sfn|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was also known as '''Naishidokoro''', referencing the sacred mirror's earlier placement in the '''Onmeiden''' before its relocation to a separate hall{{Efn2|The transition of the sacred mirror from the Emperor’s chamber to the Onmeiden, and later its designation as Naishidokoro, is detailed in several sources, such as the {{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|qid=Q217955|lt=Sarashina Nikki|1=Sarashina Nikki}} and {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|qid=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji|1=The Tale of Genji}}'s "Makibashira" chapter{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}.}}. Various kanji have been used for "Kashiko-dokoro," including '''恐所''', '''畏所''', '''威所''', '''尊所''', and '''貴所'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Daily offerings ('''Nikkū''') are conducted in the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' by the '''Naishoten''', and Imperial rituals ('''Myōchō Goyōhai''') are performed daily without interruption{{Sfn|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the '''Seasonal Festivals''' (旬祭), the '''Kashiko-dokoro Ritual of Kanname-sai''' in October, and the '''Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura Ritual''' in December are held annually{{Sfn|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == Overview == The Three Palace Sanctuaries face south, with Kashiko-dokoro in the center, Kōreiden to the west, and Shinden to the east {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=45}}. Kashiko-dokoro is considered the most noble building among the three sanctuaries, and its floor is designed to be about one shaku (approximately 30.3 cm) higher than the other two sanctuaries {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}. Each sanctuary is divided into the '''outer sanctuary''' (a room where only [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] members are allowed to enter), the '''inner sanctuary''' (a room where only the Emperor and Empress are allowed to enter), and the '''innermost sanctuary''' (a room where [[Amaterasu]] is enshrined). In the inner sanctuary of Kashiko-dokoro, there are two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|qid=Q762277|lt=eternal lamps|1=Nightlight}} {{Sfnp|皇室編集部|2017|pp=42-43}}. Historically, it was also called Naishidokoro ({{ill|内侍所|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/内侍所|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635085}}) {{Efn2|神鏡は天皇の御座所に祀られていたが、その後に別殿の[[温明殿]]に移され、内侍所とも称されるようになった{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=59}}。例えば『{{ill|Sarashina Nikki|en|Sarashina Nikki|qid=Q217955|lt=更級日記|1=Sarashina Nikki}}』に内侍所とある{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=22}}。『{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|qid=Q8269|lt=源氏物語|1=The Tale of Genji}}』の「{{ill|真木柱|ja|真木柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/真木柱|1=Makibashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583235}}」にも内侍所とあり、11月には祭祀が夥しく行われていたことが反映されている{{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|p=46}}。なお、別殿に移された時期について、『{{ill|江家次第|ja|江家次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11550820}}』や『{{ill|禁秘抄|ja|禁秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/禁秘抄|1=Kinpishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591153}}』は垂仁天皇の代とし、『{{ill|撰集抄|ja|撰集抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/撰集抄|1=Senjusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498768}}』は{{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=宇多天皇|1=Emperor Uda}}の代としている{{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|pp=59-60}}。}}. Various characters were used for "Kashiko-dokoro" such as "恐所," "畏所," "威所," "尊所," and "貴所" {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=1-2}}. Every morning, rituals are conducted by the {{ill|naishoten|ja|内掌典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naishoten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} and {{ill|Shinto ritual|en|Shinto ritual|qid=Q109937652|lt=shinji|1=Shinto ritual}} are performed by an envoy dispatched by the Emperor without fail {{Sfnp|松山能夫|1966|pp=121-123}}. Various rituals are also conducted, such as the Emperor's worship on the first day of the month, the [[Kanname-sai|Kanname-sai Kashiko-dokoro ceremony]] (a grand festival) in October, and the [[Kashiko-dokoro Misogi|Kashiko-dokoro Misogi ceremony]] (a minor festival) in December {{Sfnp|中澤伸弘|2010|pp=64-92}}. == History == The sacred mirror bestowed by [[Amaterasu]] has been enshrined within the Imperial Palace by successive generations of emperors according to the "Same Bed, Same Hall" divine decree {{Sfnp|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of the tenth Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Sujin]], the sacred mirror was moved outside the palace for enshrinement due to "living together being uneasy" {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|この神鏡は{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=垂仁天皇|1=Emperor Suinin}}の代に{{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=伊勢国|1=Ise Province}}の{{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|qid=Q6089522|lt=五十鈴川|1=Isuzu River}}上流に移され、後に{{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}となる{{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}。}}. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shuui|1=Kogo Shūi}}, at that time, a duplicate of the sacred mirror was newly cast for enshrinement in the palace {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=54}}. From the mid-Heian period onward, with the burning of the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3012148}} and other reasons, temporary palaces were established, and with them, Kashiko-dokoro was also relocated to appropriate buildings {{Sfnp|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. In later years, Kashiko-dokoro was moved to the {{ill|Shunkoden|ja|春興殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkoden|1=Shunkōden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513798}}, and according to the descriptions in the {{ill|Meimosho|ja|名目抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meimosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Toin Sanehiro|ja|洞院実熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toin Sanehiro|1=Sanehiro Tōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555877}}, by the early Muromachi period, Shunkoden had become Kashiko-dokoro {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. From the Jōkyō and Genroku periods of the Edo era, commoners were allowed to visit Naishidokoro on [[Setsubun]], and it became bustling with many visitors {{Sfnp|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In the early Meiji period, it was relocated to Tokyo, and according to a decree in September of the 4th year of Meiji (1871), shrines including Kashiko-dokoro were constructed {{Sfnp|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. == History == The sacred mirror, bestowed by '''Amaterasu''', was enshrined in the Imperial Palace per the divine command ('''Same Chamber, Same Hall''') and has been worshiped by successive emperors{{Sfn|八束清貫|1957|p=4}}. According to the '''Nihon Shoki''', during the reign of Emperor Sujin (崇神天皇), the sacred mirror was relocated outside the palace for more secure worship{{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=53}}{{Efn2|This mirror was eventually moved to the '''Ise Shrine''' during the reign of Emperor Suinin (垂仁天皇){{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=4}}.}}. The '''Kashiko-dokoro''' experienced relocations due to the destruction of the '''Dairi''' (Imperial Residence) in the Heian period and the establishment of '''temporary residences''' ('''Satodairi'''){{Sfn|橋本富太郎|2005|p=65}}. By the early Muromachi period, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' had moved to the '''Shunki-den''', as noted in '''Naimokushō''' by '''Dōin Sanehiro'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|p=12}}. During the '''Ōnin War''', the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was temporarily relocated to the '''Muromachi Palace''', where it regained its position as the priority site for Imperial rituals{{Sfn|中本真人|2021|p=52-53}}. By the Edo period, public access to the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was permitted during the '''Setsubun''' festival, attracting numerous visitors{{Sfn|藤田覚|2018|pp=207-208}}. In 1871, during the Meiji era, the '''Kashiko-dokoro''' was officially reconstructed as part of the newly relocated '''Imperial Palace'''{{Sfn|皇典講究所|1908|pp=13-15}}. ==See also== * {{ill|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|en|Kashikodokoro Riding Car|qid=Q11635084|lt=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car|1=Kashiko-dokoro Riding Car}} * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|qid=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} * {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|ja|賢所御神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinri Koban|ja|禁裏小番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinri Koban|qq=}} == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}}
